conversation_summary:
The user is continuing to refine the layout and user experience of the "Feed" and "Network" pages, with a strong emphasis on ensuring key content appears "above the fold" upon page load. Recent changes have focused on adjusting spacing and element sizes within the "Feed.jsx" and "Network.jsx" files to achieve this goal.

The user has adopted a pattern of iterative adjustments, first reducing margins and then further refining spacing and header sizes on both pages. A significant architectural change was made to the "Feed" page, restructuring its layout to mirror the simpler, centered, single-column design of the "TribalHub" page. This involved removing a sidebar and focusing the content. Additionally, functionality to automatically scroll to the top of the page upon loading has been implemented for the "Feed" page, mirroring a similar feature in "TribalHub."
